Title: ARCH_WW1_WebTier_ServerProvisioning
Step Action: Log into AWS console and navigate to EC2 Instances for the target environment
Step Expected Result: EC2 instances list is displayed

Step Action: Locate windward-web-server1 and confirm Instance Type is m5.large
Step Expected Result: Instance type shows m5.large

Step Action: Confirm windward-web-server1 OS is Windows Server 2022
Step Expected Result: Platform details show Windows 2022

Step Action: Confirm windward-web-server1 is in Availability Zone AZ1
Step Expected Result: Availability Zone matches AZ1

Step Action: Repeat all checks for windward-web-server2 (m5.large / Windows 2022 / AZ1)
Step Expected Result: windward-web-server2 matches identical spec to windward-web-server1

Step Action: Confirm both servers are in Running state with 2/2 status checks passed
Step Expected Result: Both servers show Running with no failed status checks

Title: ARCH_WW1_WebTier_DriveConfiguration
Step Action: In EC2 select windward-web-server1 and navigate to the Storage tab
Step Expected Result: Storage volumes list is displayed

Step Action: Verify C drive: 100GB gp3
Step Expected Result: C drive shows 100GB gp3 volume attached

Step Action: Verify D drive: 100GB gp3
Step Expected Result: D drive shows 100GB gp3 volume attached

Step Action: Verify P drive: 12GB gp3
Step Expected Result: P drive shows 12GB gp3 volume attached

Step Action: Repeat drive verification for windward-web-server2 (C:100GB / D:100GB / P:12GB all gp3)
Step Expected Result: windward-web-server2 drive configuration matches windward-web-server1 exactly

Title: ARCH_WW1_WebTier_LoadBalancer
Step Action: In AWS console navigate to EC2 > Load Balancers and locate windward-web-loadbalancer-alb
Step Expected Result: ALB is present and in Active state

Step Action: Confirm the load balancer type is ALB (Application Load Balancer)
Step Expected Result: Load balancer type shows Application

Step Action: Navigate to the Target Groups for windward-web-loadbalancer-alb
Step Expected Result: Target groups list is displayed

Step Action: Confirm both windward-web-server1 and windward-web-server2 are registered as targets
Step Expected Result: Both web servers appear as registered targets

Step Action: Confirm both targets show Healthy status
Step Expected Result: Both targets show healthy with no unhealthy or draining instances

Step Action: Confirm ALB DNS name and IP are documented in the Environment Variables spreadsheet
Step Expected Result: Environment Variables spreadsheet ALB-Physical-LoadBalancer field is populated and matches the ALB DNS name shown in AWS

Title: ARCH_WW1_AppTier_ServerProvisioning
Step Action: Log into AWS console and navigate to EC2 Instances for the target environment
Step Expected Result: EC2 instances list is displayed

Step Action: Locate windward-app-server1 and confirm Instance Type is m5.large and OS is Windows 2022
Step Expected Result: Instance type is m5.large and platform shows Windows 2022

Step Action: Confirm windward-app-server1 is in Availability Zone AZ1
Step Expected Result: AZ matches AZ1

Step Action: Confirm windward-app-server2 is m5.large / Windows 2022 / AZ1
Step Expected Result: windward-app-server2 matches expected spec

Step Action: Confirm windward-app-server3 is m5.large / Windows 2022 / AZ1
Step Expected Result: windward-app-server3 matches expected spec

Step Action: Confirm all three app servers are in Running state with 2/2 status checks passed
Step Expected Result: All three servers show Running with no failed status checks

Title: ARCH_WW1_AppTier_DriveConfiguration
Step Action: In EC2 select windward-app-server1 and navigate to the Storage tab
Step Expected Result: Storage volumes list is displayed

Step Action: Verify C drive: 100GB gp3 / D drive: 100GB gp3 / P drive: 12GB gp3
Step Expected Result: All three volumes match expected sizes and type

Step Action: Repeat for windward-app-server2: C:100GB gp3 / D:100GB gp3 / P:12GB gp3
Step Expected Result: windward-app-server2 drive configuration matches spec

Step Action: Verify windward-app-server3: C:100GB gp3 / D:100GB gp3 / P:8GB gp3
Step Expected Result: windward-app-server3 P drive is 8GB gp3 (smaller than server1 and server2)

Title: ARCH_WW1_AppTier_LoadBalancers
Step Action: In AWS console navigate to EC2 > Load Balancers and locate windward-app-NLB-loadbalancer
Step Expected Result: NLB is present and in Active state

Step Action: Confirm type is NLB (Network Load Balancer)
Step Expected Result: Load balancer type shows Network

Step Action: Confirm windward-app-server2 and windward-app-server3 are registered as healthy targets in the NLB
Step Expected Result: Both servers show as healthy targets; windward-app-server1 (Utility) is NOT a target of the NLB

Step Action: Locate windward-app-ALB-loadbalancer and confirm type is ALB
Step Expected Result: Load balancer type shows Application

Step Action: Confirm windward-app-server2 and windward-app-server3 are registered as healthy targets in the App ALB
Step Expected Result: Both servers show as healthy targets

Step Action: Locate windward-app-utility-ALB-loadbalancer and confirm windward-app-server1 is the sole registered healthy target
Step Expected Result: Only windward-app-server1 appears as a registered target with healthy status

Step Action: Confirm NLB and ALB DNS names and IPs are documented in the Environment Variables spreadsheet
Step Expected Result: windward-app-NLB-loadbalancer and ALB-Physical-LoadBalancer fields are populated in the spreadsheet

Title: ARCH_WW1_AppTier_UtilityServerApplications
Step Action: RDP or connect to windward-app-server1 (Utility) and verify the following APIs are installed and running: Group API / Member API / PCD API
Step Expected Result: All three APIs are present and running as IIS applications or Windows services with no stopped or errored state

Step Action: Verify DataHub API (V4 / V5 / V6 variants) are installed on windward-app-server1
Step Expected Result: DataHub API versions are deployed and accessible on the Utility server

Step Action: Verify the SSIS packages and TWS agent are installed on windward-app-server1
Step Expected Result: SSIS is installed; TWS agent service is present and running on app-server1

Step Action: Verify app-server2 and app-server3 host the full Windward app tier application set (non-utility apps)
Step Expected Result: Windward app tier applications are deployed and running on both app-server2 and app-server3

Title: ARCH_WW2_ServerProvisioning
Step Action: Log into AWS console and navigate to EC2 Instances
Step Expected Result: EC2 instances list is displayed

Step Action: Locate windward2-web-server1 and confirm Instance Type is m5.large / OS Windows 2022 / AZ1
Step Expected Result: Instance spec matches: m5.large

Step Action: Confirm windward2-web-server1 drives: C:100GB gp3 / D:100GB gp3 / P:12GB gp3
Step Expected Result: All three drives match expected sizes and storage type

Step Action: Locate windward2-app-server1 and confirm Instance Type is m5.large / OS Windows 2022 / AZ1
Step Expected Result: Instance spec matches: m5.large

Step Action: Confirm windward2-app-server1 drives: C:100GB gp3 / D:100GB gp3 / P:12GB gp3
Step Expected Result: All three drives match expected sizes and storage type

Step Action: Confirm both windward2 servers are Running with 2/2 status checks passed
Step Expected Result: Both servers are healthy with no failed status checks

Title: ARCH_WW2_Applications
Step Action: Connect to windward2-web-server1 and confirm the Windward 2.0 Payment web application is installed and running
Step Expected Result: Payment application is deployed and accessible on windward2-web-server1

Step Action: Confirm the Windward 2.0 Config (Configuration) web application is installed and running on windward2-web-server1
Step Expected Result: Config application is deployed and accessible on windward2-web-server1

Step Action: Connect to windward2-app-server1 and confirm Payment and Config app tier components are installed and running
Step Expected Result: Both application tier components are present and running on windward2-app-server1

Step Action: Verify windward2-web-server1 DNS aliases are configured: payment-webserver-alias and config-webserver-alias both resolve to windward2-web-server1 IP
Step Expected Result: Both DNS aliases resolve to the correct server IP documented in the Environment Variables spreadsheet

Step Action: Verify windward2-web-server1 connects through windward2-app-server1 which connects to the WW1 Database Tier
Step Expected Result: Traffic flow follows the documented path: WW2 Web → WW2 App → WW1 DB Tier

Title: ARCH_DB_PrimaryServerProvisioning
Step Action: Log into AWS console and locate windward-database-server in EC2 Instances
Step Expected Result: Server is present and in Running state

Step Action: Confirm Instance Type is m5.2xlarge and OS is Windows Server 2022
Step Expected Result: Instance type shows m5.2xlarge and platform shows Windows 2022

Step Action: Confirm Availability Zone is AZ1
Step Expected Result: Server is deployed in AZ1

Step Action: Navigate to Storage tab and verify C:100GB / D:600GB / E:400GB / H:50GB / L:250GB / S:50GB / T:250GB / P:40GB / U:100GB (all gp3)
Step Expected Result: All 9 drive volumes are present with correct sizes and gp3 storage type

Step Action: Confirm SQL Server Standard edition is installed on windward-database-server
Step Expected Result: SQL Server version shows Standard edition

Title: ARCH_DB_FailoverServerProvisioning
Step Action: Locate windward-database-failover-server in EC2 Instances
Step Expected Result: Server is present and in Running state

Step Action: Confirm Instance Type is m5.2xlarge / OS Windows 2022 / Availability Zone AZ2
Step Expected Result: All three specs match — note AZ2 (different AZ from primary for HA)

Step Action: Verify drives: C:100GB / D:600GB / E:400GB / L:250GB / S:50GB / T:250GB / U:100GB (all gp3)
Step Expected Result: Drive configuration matches expected spec

Step Action: Confirm SQL Server Standard edition is installed
Step Expected Result: SQL Server version shows Standard edition

Title: ARCH_DB_ReadOnlyServerProvisioning
Step Action: Locate windward-database-read-only-server in EC2 Instances
Step Expected Result: Server is present and in Running state

Step Action: Confirm Instance Type is m5.2xlarge / OS Windows 2022 / Availability Zone AZ1
Step Expected Result: All three specs match

Step Action: Verify drives: C:100GB / D:600GB / E:1.5TB / L:250GB / S:50GB / T:250GB / U:100GB (all gp3) — note E drive is 1.5TB, larger than primary
Step Expected Result: All drives present with correct sizes; E drive is 1.5TB gp3

Step Action: Confirm SQL Server Standard edition is installed
Step Expected Result: SQL Server version shows Standard edition

Step Action: Locate windward-database-read-only-failover-server and confirm matching spec in AZ2
Step Expected Result: Read-only failover server matches read-only server spec and is in AZ2

Title: ARCH_DB_DistributionServerProvisioning
Step Action: Locate windward-database-distribution-server in EC2 Instances
Step Expected Result: Server is present and in Running state

Step Action: Confirm Instance Type is m5.2xlarge / OS Windows 2022 / Availability Zone AZ2
Step Expected Result: All three specs match; note AZ2

Step Action: Verify drives: C:100GB / D:500GB / L:250GB / T:250GB / U:250GB / H:50GB / S:50GB (all gp3)
Step Expected Result: All 7 drives present with correct sizes; note D drive is 500GB (smaller than primary) and U drive is 250GB (larger than primary)

Step Action: Confirm SQL Server Standard edition is installed and configured for replication distribution
Step Expected Result: SQL Server shows Standard edition and Distributor role is configured

Title: ARCH_DB_DatabaseInventory
Step Action: Connect to windward-database-server (primary) and open SQL Server Management Studio or equivalent
Step Expected Result: Connection to primary database server succeeds

Step Action: Verify all required databases are present: ApplicationSecurity, CommonDB, Core, Core_Logging, Windward_Commercial, EDIdb, NSB_Config, NSB_Payment, Windward_Config, WindwardInterfaces_COM
Step Expected Result: All 10 databases are listed in the database inventory with Online status

Step Action: Verify shell databases are present: TempDB_WW, XREF, WindwardInterfaces
Step Expected Result: All 3 shell databases are present

Step Action: Verify Windward_Payment_COM is present (created by Application Services team)
Step Expected Result: Windward_Payment_COM database exists and is accessible

Step Action: Verify ASPState database is present (created during application install)
Step Expected Result: ASPState database is present

Title: ARCH_DB_AvailabilityGroups
Step Action: Connect to windward-database-server and navigate to Always On Availability Groups in SQL Server
Step Expected Result: Availability Groups configuration is visible

Step Action: Verify an individual Basic Availability Group exists for each required database: WW, AS, CMN, Core, CLog, NSB_Cfg, NSB_Pmt, TempDB_WW, WW_Cfg, WW_Intrf, WW_Intrf_Com, XREF, Edi (each suffixed with Tenant ID)
Step Expected Result: All 13 Availability Groups are present with names following the format DB_[TenantID]

Step Action: Verify each Availability Group shows Primary replica on windward-database-server (AZ1)
Step Expected Result: All AGs show windward-database-server as the Primary replica

Step Action: Verify each Availability Group shows Secondary replica on windward-database-failover-server (AZ2)
Step Expected Result: All AGs show windward-database-failover-server as the Secondary replica in AZ2

Step Action: Verify all Availability Groups show Synchronized state with no health warnings
Step Expected Result: All AGs show Synchronized status with no critical or warning states

Title: ARCH_DB_ReadOnlyAvailabilityGroups
Step Action: Connect to windward-database-read-only-server and navigate to Always On Availability Groups
Step Expected Result: Read-only Availability Groups are visible (WW_R, XREF_R, CMN_R, Core_R, TempDB_WW_R, WW_Cfg_R suffixed with Tenant ID)

Step Action: Verify all 6 read-only AGs are present: WW, XREF, CMN, Core, TempDB_WW, WW_Cfg (each suffixed with Tenant ID and R)
Step Expected Result: All 6 read-only AGs are listed

Step Action: Verify each read-only AG shows Primary on windward-database-read-only-server (AZ1) and Secondary on windward-database-read-only-failover-server (AZ2)
Step Expected Result: All read-only AGs show correct primary/secondary replica assignment

Step Action: Confirm all read-only AGs are in Synchronized state
Step Expected Result: All read-only AGs show Synchronized with no health warnings

Title: ARCH_DB_Replication
Step Action: Connect to windward-database-distribution-server and verify the Distributor role is configured
Step Expected Result: Distribution database is present and Distributor is active

Step Action: Verify that the following databases are configured for replication from windward-database-server to windward-database-read-only-server: CommonDB, Core, Windward_Commercial, Windward_Config
Step Expected Result: All 4 databases appear as published databases in the replication topology

Step Action: Verify shell databases TempDB_WW and XREF are replicated
Step Expected Result: Both shell databases appear in the replication topology

Step Action: Check Replication Monitor and confirm all publications show a Replication Status of Running with no errors or warnings
Step Expected Result: All publications show Running status with no latency alerts or errors

Step Action: Confirm all tables with a primary key are included in the replication articles for each published database
Step Expected Result: Replication article configuration includes all tables with primary keys for each publication

Title: ARCH_DB_FailoverCluster
Step Action: Connect to windward-database-server and open Windows Server Failover Cluster Manager
Step Expected Result: Cluster Manager opens and the cluster for windward-database-server is visible

Step Action: Verify the cluster has two nodes: windward-database-server (AZ1) and windward-database-failover-server (AZ2)
Step Expected Result: Both nodes appear in the cluster with Online status

Step Action: Verify the Cluster IP and AG Listener IP are configured and match the values documented in the Environment Variables spreadsheet (cluster1 and listener1)
Step Expected Result: Cluster IP and Listener IP match the spreadsheet values

Step Action: Confirm the Quorum is configured to the FSW path: \\amznfsxrwhfzi9b\share\Quorum\
Step Expected Result: Quorum witness path matches the documented share path

Step Action: Repeat cluster verification for the read-only cluster: windward-database-read-only-server (AZ1) + windward-database-read-only-failover-server (AZ2)
Step Expected Result: Read-only cluster shows both nodes Online with Listener configured (cluster2 and listener2)

Title: ARCH_DB_BackupShare
Step Action: From windward-database-server confirm access to the backup share: \\amznfsxxhwvhskd.dqdev.ad\share\SQL_Backups
Step Expected Result: Backup share path is accessible from the database server

Step Action: Verify transaction log backup jobs are scheduled and have executed successfully
Step Expected Result: SQL Agent jobs for transaction log backups are present and last execution shows Succeeded

Step Action: Confirm backup files are present in the share for the primary databases
Step Expected Result: Backup files exist in the share directory with recent timestamps

Title: ARCH_Security_AllAppAndWebServers
Step Action: For each web and app server (windward-web-server1, windward-web-server2, windward-app-server1, windward-app-server2, windward-app-server3, windward2-web-server1, windward2-app-server1) confirm SentinelOne agent is installed and active
Step Expected Result: SentinelOne agent shows as Active on all 7 servers

Step Action: Confirm Qualys agent is installed and reporting on all 7 servers
Step Expected Result: Qualys agent shows as active and last scan time is recent

Step Action: Confirm Imperva agent is installed and active on all 7 servers
Step Expected Result: Imperva agent is present and active

Step Action: Confirm Forcepoint agent is installed and active on all 7 servers
Step Expected Result: Forcepoint agent is present and active

Step Action: Confirm Exabeam agent is installed and active on all 7 servers
Step Expected Result: Exabeam agent is present and active

Title: ARCH_Security_DatabaseServers
Step Action: For each database server (windward-database-server, failover, read-only, read-only-failover, distribution) confirm SentinelOne / Qualys / Imperva / Forcepoint / Exabeam agents are installed and active
Step Expected Result: All 5 security agents show Active on all 5 database servers

Step Action: Confirm Illumio agent is installed and active on all database servers (database servers have an additional security control)
Step Expected Result: Illumio agent shows as active on all 5 database servers

Title: ARCH_Integration_WW1CoreChain
Step Action: Access the Windward Web application URL (via windward-web-loadbalancer-alb alias) and perform a login
Step Expected Result: Login succeeds confirming Web Tier → App Tier → Database Tier chain is functioning

Step Action: Perform a data-driven operation (e.g. member search) that requires a round-trip through Web → App → DB
Step Expected Result: Operation returns data successfully confirming all three tiers are communicating

Step Action: Confirm the Web Tier is routing requests through the NLB (windward-app-NLB-loadbalancer) to app-server2 and app-server3
Step Expected Result: Traffic reaches app-server2 and app-server3 via the NLB (confirm via access logs or health check)

Step Action: Confirm utility operations (Group API / Member API) are being served from windward-app-server1 via windward-app-utility-ALB-loadbalancer
Step Expected Result: Utility API endpoints are accessible through the utility ALB and served from app-server1

Title: ARCH_Integration_WW2Chain
Step Action: Access the Windward 2.0 Payment web application URL (via payment-webserver-alias) and confirm it loads
Step Expected Result: Payment application loads successfully

Step Action: Access the Windward 2.0 Config web application URL (via config-webserver-alias) and confirm it loads
Step Expected Result: Config application loads successfully

Step Action: Perform a data-driven operation in each WW2 application that requires a database call
Step Expected Result: Operations return data confirming WW2 Web → WW2 App → WW1 DB Tier chain is functioning

Title: ARCH_Integration_NextGenServices
Step Action: Confirm Business Services Lambda is reachable and returns a healthy response (via Postman or health endpoint)
Step Expected Result: Business Services health check passes

Step Action: Confirm Member Domain Lambda is reachable and health check passes
Step Expected Result: Member Domain health check passes

Step Action: Confirm Claims Domain Lambda is reachable and health check passes
Step Expected Result: Claims Domain health check passes

Step Action: Confirm Member Benefit Service is reachable and health check passes
Step Expected Result: Member Benefit Service health check passes

Step Action: Confirm Business Services connects to Member Domain and Claims Domain by reviewing service logs or Postman integration test
Step Expected Result: Logs or Postman results confirm BS → MD and BS → CD connections are successful

Title: ARCH_Integration_APIConnectivity
Step Action: Confirm Member API is accessible via its endpoint on windward-app-server1 and can query the Core database
Step Expected Result: Member API returns a valid response and database query succeeds

Step Action: Confirm Group API is accessible via its endpoint on windward-app-server1 and can query the Core database
Step Expected Result: Group API returns a valid response and database query succeeds

Step Action: Confirm Config application (WW2) can connect to the Windward_Config database on the WW1 DB Tier
Step Expected Result: Config app successfully connects to Windward_Config with no connection errors in logs

Step Action: Confirm Payment application (WW2) can connect to the Windward_Payment_COM database on the WW1 DB Tier
Step Expected Result: Payment app successfully connects to Windward_Payment_COM with no connection errors in logs

Title: ARCH_Integration_OnPremConnectivity
Step Action: Confirm the Windward DB Tier can write to the on-premises DataHub (OnPremDataHub) by reviewing replication or export job logs
Step Expected Result: Logs confirm successful data flow from DB Tier to OnPremDataHub with no connectivity errors

Step Action: Confirm NextGen services can reach OnPremServices (AsyncService and PaperlessService) from the AWS Pilot environment
Step Expected Result: Service connectivity confirmed via health check or log review with no timeout or connection refused errors

Title: ARCH_JobTier_SSISAndTWS
Step Action: Connect to windward-app-server1 (Utility) and verify the TWS agent service is installed
Step Expected Result: TWS agent service is present in Windows Services

Step Action: Confirm the TWS agent service is Running and connected to the on-premises TWS server
Step Expected Result: Service status shows Running; TWS server connection is established

Step Action: Confirm SSIS packages are deployed and scheduled on windward-app-server1
Step Expected Result: SSIS packages are present in SQL Server Agent or SSIS catalog on app-server1

Step Action: Execute a test SSIS package (or review last execution history) and confirm it ran successfully
Step Expected Result: SSIS package execution shows Succeeded in execution history with no errors

Step Action: In AWS EC2 confirm windward-web-server1 and windward-web-server2 are both in AZ1

Step Action: Confirm windward-app-server1, windward-app-server2, windward-app-server3 are all in AZ1
Step Expected Result: All three app servers show AZ1

Step Action: Confirm windward2-web-server1 and windward2-app-server1 are in AZ1
Step Expected Result: Both WW2 servers show AZ1

Step Action: Confirm windward-database-server and windward-database-read-only-server are in AZ1
Step Expected Result: Primary and read-only database servers are in AZ1

Step Action: Confirm windward-database-failover-server and windward-database-read-only-failover-server are in AZ2
